meta.description: Compressed hunt cards for general-security — one-line detection rules for bulk scanning
meta.usage: Load cards into context. For each card, grep target code for card.grep. On hit: execute card.check steps directly, use validWhen/invalidWhen/impact for rapid triage, and only read full DB entry (card.ref + card.lines) for confirmed true/likely positives.

cards.title: Pattern 1: Unprotected Mint/Burn Functions (CRITICAL)
check: VERIFY: Real Exploit: SafeMoon (2023-03, $8.9M)
PROVE: Anyone can burn tokens from any address
cards.antipattern: Anyone can burn tokens from any address
cards.validWhen: Real Exploit: SafeMoon (2023-03, $8.9M)

cards.title: Pattern 2: Unprotected Initialization (CRITICAL)
check: VERIFY: Real Exploit: DAO Maker (2021-09, $4M)
PROVE: init function can be called by anyone
cards.antipattern: init function can be called by anyone
cards.validWhen: Real Exploit: DAO Maker (2021-09, $4M)

cards.title: Pattern 3: Arbitrary External Call (CRITICAL)
check: VERIFY: Real Exploit: SocketGateway (2024-01, $3.3M)
PROVE: Performs arbitrary call with user-controlled data
cards.antipattern: Performs arbitrary call with user-controlled data
cards.validWhen: Real Exploit: SocketGateway (2024-01, $3.3M)

cards.title: Pattern 4: Missing msg.sender Validation (HIGH)
check: VERIFY: Real Exploit: ChaingeFinance (2024-04, $560K)
PROVE: Doesn't verify caller has authority over tokenAddr
cards.antipattern: Doesn't verify caller has authority over tokenAddr
cards.validWhen: Real Exploit: ChaingeFinance (2024-04, $560K)

cards.title: Pattern 5: Unprotected Critical Parameter Updates (HIGH)
check: VERIFY: Real Exploit: SuperRare (2025-07, $730K)
PROVE: Anyone can update merkle root
cards.validWhen: Real Exploit: SuperRare (2025-07, $730K)

cards.title: Pattern 6: Unprotected Internal Functions Made External (HIGH)
check: VERIFY: Real Exploit: LeetSwap (2023-08, $630K)
PROVE: Internal helper exposed as external
cards.validWhen: Real Exploit: LeetSwap (2023-08, $630K)

cards.title: Pattern 7: MEV Bot Access Control (HIGH)
check: VERIFY: Real Exploits: Multiple MEV Bots (2023, $2.5M+)
PROVE: Checks msg.sender instead of tx.origin or uses weak checks
cards.antipattern: Checks msg.sender instead of tx.origin or uses weak checks
cards.validWhen: Real Exploits: Multiple MEV Bots (2023, $2.5M+)

cards.title: Fix 3: Validate External Calls
check: VERIFY: contract SecureRouter {
FALSIFY: Never allow transferFrom on arbitrary tokens
cards.securePattern: Never allow transferFrom on arbitrary tokens
cards.validWhen: contract SecureRouter {
cards.invalidWhen: Never allow transferFrom on arbitrary tokens

cards.title: Fix 4: Validate msg.sender Authority
check: VERIFY: contract SecureVault {
PROVE: Allows operating on any user
FALSIFY: Only allow operating on own balance
cards.securePattern: Only allow operating on own balance
cards.validWhen: contract SecureVault {
cards.invalidWhen: Only allow operating on own balance

cards.title: Fix 5: Protect Critical Parameter Updates
check: VERIFY: contract SecureStaking {
FALSIFY: Only admin can update merkle root
cards.securePattern: Only admin can update merkle root
cards.validWhen: contract SecureStaking {
cards.invalidWhen: Only admin can update merkle root

cards.title: Invariant Checks
cards.detect: INV-AC-001: Only the contract owner can withdraw contract funds
check: VERIFY: INV-AC-001: Only the contract owner can withdraw contract funds
cards.validWhen: INV-AC-001: Only the contract owner can withdraw contract funds

cards.title: 1. Public Mint + Public Burn on Token Contract (SafeMoon $8.9M)
cards.detect: SafeMoon's token contract had `mint()` and `burn()` functions that were callable by anyone — no `onlyOwner` or role check.
check: VERIFY: SafeMoon's token contract had mint() and burn() functions that were callable by anyone — no onlyOwner or role check.
PROVE: Flash loan WBNB; Swap WBNB → SafeMoon via PancakeSwap
ANTIPATTERN: mint() and burn() have NO access control
cards.antipattern: mint() and burn() have NO access control
cards.validWhen: SafeMoon's token contract had mint() and burn() functions that were callable by anyone — no onlyOwner or role check.

cards.title: 2. Public Fee Transfer Function on DEX Pair (LeetSwap $630K)
cards.detect: LeetSwap's pair contract had a public `_transferFeesSupportingTaxTokens()` function that allowed anyone to transfer tokens out of the pair as "fees".
check: VERIFY: LeetSwap's pair contract had a public _transferFeesSupportingTaxTokens() function that allowed anyone to transfer tokens out of the pair as "fees".
PROVE: _transferFeesSupportingTaxTokens is PUBLIC
cards.validWhen: LeetSwap's pair contract had a public _transferFeesSupportingTaxTokens() function that allowed anyone to transfer tokens out of the pair as "fees".

cards.title: 3. Aggregator Arbitrary-Sender Swap (SwapX $1M)
cards.detect: SwapX aggregator's swap function accepted a `from` address parameter, allowing the caller to initiate swaps using...
check: VERIFY: SwapX aggregator's swap function accepted a from address parameter, allowing the caller to initiate swaps using another user's pre-approved tokens.
PROVE: Swap function uses caller-specified 'from' address
cards.validWhen: SwapX aggregator's swap function accepted a from address parameter, allowing the caller to initiate swaps using another user's pre-approved tokens.

cards.title: 4. Token Lock/Claim with Migration Vulnerability (SHIDO $230K)
cards.detect: SHIDO's token migration system allowed users to lock SHIDOINU tokens and claim SHIDO tokens.
check: VERIFY: SHIDO's token migration system allowed users to lock SHIDOINU tokens and claim SHIDO tokens.
PROVE: Lock contract mishandles migration rate
cards.validWhen: SHIDO's token migration system allowed users to lock SHIDOINU tokens and claim SHIDO tokens.

cards.title: 5. Router/Bot Authorization Failures (Maestro $630K, UniBot $84K)
cards.detect: Trading bot routers (MaestroRouter, UniBot) processed swap transactions on behalf of users but failed to properly...
check: VERIFY: Trading bot routers (MaestroRouter, UniBot) processed swap transactions on behalf of users but failed to properly verify that the calling address was...
PROVE: Router processes swaps without verifying authorization
cards.antipattern: Router processes swaps without verifying authorization
cards.validWhen: Trading bot routers (MaestroRouter, UniBot) processed swap transactions on behalf of users but failed to properly verify that the calling address was authorized to...

cards.title: 1. Missing Access Control on Module Initialization
cards.detect: When a protocol's core module initialization function (`initializeModuleCore()`) lacks an access control modifier (e.
check: VERIFY: When a protocol's core module initialization function (initializeModuleCore()) lacks an access control modifier (e.g., onlyOwner, onlyAdmin, or...
PROVE: Find initializeModuleCore() — verify it lacks access control; Call it with attacker-controlled addresses for admin, token, and config parameters
ANTIPATTERN: initializeModuleCore() has no access control
cards.validWhen: When a protocol's core module initialization function (initializeModuleCore()) lacks an access control modifier (e.g., onlyOwner, onlyAdmin, or initializer), any...

cards.title: 2. Missing Caller Restriction on Core Execution Function
cards.detect: When a DEX or AMM exposes an `execute()` function that performs token swaps, liquidity operations, and balance...
check: VERIFY: When a DEX or AMM exposes an execute() function that performs token swaps, liquidity operations, and balance adjustments without validating that the...
PROVE: Find the core velocoreexecute() function — no caller restriction; Encode token operations with type(int128).max amounts (maximum withdrawal)
ANTIPATTERN: velocore__execute() callable by anyone
cards.validWhen: When a DEX or AMM exposes an execute() function that performs token swaps, liquidity operations, and balance adjustments without validating that the caller is an...

cards.title: 3. Public Mint Function on Collateral Token
cards.detect: When a protocol's collateral token contract exposes a `mint()` function without an `onlyOwner` or `onlyMinter`...
check: VERIFY: When a protocol's collateral token contract exposes a mint() function without an onlyOwner or onlyMinter modifier, anyone can mint unlimited tokens.
PROVE: Identify the collateral token contract — find public mint() function; Mint a large amount of collateral tokens to attacker's address
ANTIPATTERN: ShezmuUSD.mint() has no access control
cards.validWhen: When a protocol's collateral token contract exposes a mint() function without an onlyOwner or onlyMinter modifier, anyone can mint unlimited tokens.

cards.title: 4. Unverified Contract with External Transfer Proxy
cards.detect: Unverified (non-open-source) contracts on blockchain explorers can hide malicious or vulnerable logic.
check: VERIFY: Unverified (non-open-source) contracts on blockchain explorers can hide malicious or vulnerable logic.
PROVE: Unverified contract with externally callable transfer logic
cards.antipattern: Unverified contract with externally callable transfer logic
cards.validWhen: Unverified (non-open-source) contracts on blockchain explorers can hide malicious or vulnerable logic.

cards.title: 5. Missing msg.sender Validation in Pool Exit
cards.detect: When a liquidity pool's `exitPool()` function accepts a `user` parameter to specify whose liquidity to withdraw but...
check: VERIFY: When a liquidity pool's exitPool() function accepts a user parameter to specify whose liquidity to withdraw but doesn't validate that msg.sender ==...
PROVE: Call exitPool(victim, maxAmount) — specify victim's address as the user; Protocol withdraws victim's liquidity and sends tokens to msg.sender (or to user parameter)
ANTIPATTERN: exitPool() doesn't validate msg.sender == user
cards.validWhen: When a liquidity pool's exitPool() function accepts a user parameter to specify whose liquidity to withdraw but doesn't validate that msg.sender == user, any caller can...

cards.title: 1. Unvalidated callTo/callData in Swap/Route Structs
cards.detect: Many DeFi protocols (routers, aggregators, bridges) accept user-supplied swap instructions containing a `callTo`...
check: VERIFY: Many DeFi protocols (routers, aggregators, bridges) accept user-supplied swap instructions containing a callTo (target address) and callData (raw...
PROVE: Identify a protocol with user approvals (router, aggregator, bridge gateway); Find a function that passes user-supplied callTo/callData to a low-level call
ANTIPATTERN: Newly added route (ID 406) passes swapExtraData to low-level call
cards.antipattern: Newly added route (ID 406) passes swapExtraData to low-level call
cards.validWhen: Many DeFi protocols (routers, aggregators, bridges) accept user-supplied swap instructions containing a callTo (target address) and callData (raw bytes) field.

cards.title: 2. Unrestricted External Call Actions (OPERATION_CALL)
cards.detect: Multi-action contracts (like Kashi/Chamberss) implement an `OPERATION_CALL` action type that allows calling arbitrary...
check: VERIFY: Multi-action contracts (like Kashi/Chamberss) implement an OPERATION_CALL action type that allows calling arbitrary external contracts.
PROVE: Chamber.performOperations() has OPERATION_CALL (opcode 30)
cards.validWhen: Multi-action contracts (like Kashi/Chamberss) implement an OPERATION_CALL action type that allows calling arbitrary external contracts.

cards.title: 3. Bridge Signer Validation Bypass
cards.detect: Cross-chain bridges use multi-signature validation on withdrawal requests — requiring N of M authorized validators to...
check: VERIFY: Cross-chain bridges use multi-signature validation on withdrawal requests — requiring N of M authorized validators to sign.
PROVE: Bridge withdraw() accepts invalid/compromised validator signatures
cards.antipattern: Bridge withdraw() accepts invalid/compromised validator signatures
cards.validWhen: Cross-chain bridges use multi-signature validation on withdrawal requests — requiring N of M authorized validators to sign.

cards.title: 4. Yul Integer Overflow Calldata Corruption
cards.detect: Contracts using Yul assembly for calldata manipulation are vulnerable to integer overflow, as Yul's `add()` opcode does...
check: VERIFY: Contracts using Yul assembly for calldata manipulation are vulnerable to integer overflow, as Yul's add() opcode does NOT revert on overflow (unlike...
PROVE: Settlement._settleOrder() uses Yul add() for calldata offsets
cards.antipattern: Settlement._settleOrder() uses Yul add() for calldata offsets
cards.validWhen: Contracts using Yul assembly for calldata manipulation are vulnerable to integer overflow, as Yul's add() opcode does NOT revert on overflow (unlike Solidity 0.8+).

cards.title: 5. Unverified Aggregator Proxy Forwarding
cards.detect: Trade aggregator proxies that accept raw calldata and forward it to external contracts create a universal call gadget.
check: VERIFY: Trade aggregator proxies that accept raw calldata and forward it to external contracts create a universal call gadget.
PROVE: Unverified aggregator proxy at 0xd3f64BAa732061F8B3626ee44bab354f854877AC
cards.validWhen: Trade aggregator proxies that accept raw calldata and forward it to external contracts create a universal call gadget.

cards.title: 1. Unvalidated Router Address + Calldata in Swap Functions
cards.detect: Swap aggregator and cross-chain proxy contracts often need to interact with multiple DEX routers.
check: VERIFY: Swap aggregator and cross-chain proxy contracts often need to interact with multiple DEX routers.
PROVE: Enumerate all addresses that approved tokens to the vulnerable router contract; For each victim, check their approved token balance
ANTIPATTERN: routerCallNative accepts ANY router address and ANY calldata
cards.antipattern: routerCallNative accepts ANY router address and ANY calldata
cards.validWhen: Swap aggregator and cross-chain proxy contracts often need to interact with multiple DEX routers.

cards.title: 5. SIGNATURE & NONCE VALIDATION PATTERNS

FALSIFY: Proper nonce validation
